calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-11-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Helen Milowe and Collin Kelley for Mary-Louise Kidd (Administrator).
Sarah Flanagan for Stanford University (Claimant).

The Court stated that the issue has to do with the extraordinary Attorney fees, that Ms. Milowe's time does not segregate extraordinary time from ordinary time and questions whether some of Mr. Kelley's time is covered by statutory fees. Ms. Milowe stated that the Court is correct, that she did not keep her time separated, that much of her time was at the beginning of the matter and that she did not anticipate ending up with litigation or that it would be so long. Ms. Milowe also stated that she went through her time records and tried to break down her time, and that she put it in her declaration. The Court asked Ms. Milowe if she wants to do a supplement or if she will accept that the Court will do the best it can to determine an appropriate amount of fees. Mr. Kelley stated that this was a surprisingly complex matter that went on much longer, that there was a lot of cross over, that it is hard to segregate between the two and that the amount of work involved was substantial and went beyond statutory fees. The Petition for Approval of Settlement of Stanford Creditor's Claim and Final Distribution on Approval of Second and Final Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Sarah Flanagan for Stanford University (Claimant)
Colin Kelley for Mary Louise Kidd (Respondent)

Mr. Kelley stated there are no additional claims against the estate, any new claims would be time barred, there are sufficient funds to pay Stanford per settlement agreement, and the administrator has authority to approve creditor's claims. Counsel agreed that the settlement agreement terminates the 850 petition. The petition requesting order pursuant to Probate Code 850 is Off Calendar. The Court granted the petition to approve settlement agreement (order signed in open court).

Petition Requesting Order Pursuant To Probate Code Section 850
Off Calendar Jan-23-2018 Continued To Apr-24-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Frank Cialone for Luc Verelst, Nelly Verelst and Peter Bang.
Collin Kelley for Mary Louise Kidd (Administrator).
Sara Flanagan for Standford University (Claimant/Objector).

Mr. Cialone stated that he would like to amend their petition to include the creditor's claims and that they are asking the Administrator to deny Standford's claim. Mr. Cialone also stated that he has conducted some discovery and that he intends to do some more. Mr. Kelley and Ms. Flanagan stated that they are open to mediation. The Court noted that the matter does not qualify for Pro Bono Mediation. Mr. Kelley spoke of providing an accounting by February 9, 2018. The Petition Requesting Order Pursuant to Probate Code Section 850 is continued to April 24, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that discovery is completed by then. The parties are to meet and confer, and mediate if that's the direction they choose to go. The parties are to also have a plan in place as to how they will proceed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition Requesting Order Pursuant To Probate Code Section 850
Off Calendar Apr-04-2017 Continued To Jul-11-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Frank Cialone for Peter Bang, Nelly Verelst-Boets and Luc Verelst (Petitioners).
Sarah Flanagan for Stanford University (Objector).
Helen Milowe and Collin Kelley for Mary Kidd (Personal Representative).

Mr. Cialone stated that the parties have not had discussion since filing of the petition, suggesting coming back, doing some discovery and stated that the Administration is back East and his Client is in Europe and this will give them time to talk. Mr. Kelley stated that they have no objection to allow time for discovery and that the party who brought the petition is best suited to present evidence. The Court raised the issue of the need to increase bond. The Petition Requesting Order Pursuant to Probate Code Section 850 is continued to July 11, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that the parties will talk to each other as to whether there is a need to do extensive discovery, that the parties will get discovery done and determine what needs to be done to proceed. Status on filing of an ex parte petition to increase bond is set for April 25, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.
